expectations.text: A test list or plan of test cases is created before any test code
expectations.evidence: No test list comment or planning step in strutil_test.go. Tests start directly at line 9 with TestTruncate_NoTruncationWhenWithinMaxLen. No mention of planning in transcript.

expectations.text: Test file (strutil_test.go) is created before implementation file (strutil.go)
expectations.evidence: Transcript shows 'Creating detailed failing tests' (msg 8) before 'Implementing basic word wrap' (msg 13). Test file was written first.

expectations.text: Tests use Arrange-Act-Assert structure with clear sections
expectations.evidence: Tests are one-liner assertions with no separation. Example: TestTruncate_NoTruncationWhenWithinMaxLen (lines 9-12) has two inline assertions: assert.Equal(t, 'hello', Truncate('hello', 10)). No arrange/act separation — call and assertion are combined.

expectations.text: At least 5 distinct test cases for Truncate covering degenerate, happy, and edge cases
expectations.evidence: Only 3 Truncate test functions. TestTruncate_NoTruncationWhenWithinMaxLen, TestTruncate_TruncatesAndAddsEllipsis (1 case), TestTruncate_ZeroOrNegativeMaxLenReturnsEmptyString.

expectations.text: At least 4 distinct test cases for WordWrap covering degenerate, happy, and edge cases
expectations.evidence: Only 3 WordWrap test functions: WrapsAtWordBoundaries, DoesNotSplitSingleLongWord, EmptyInputReturnsEmptyString.

expectations.text: Each test function tests exactly one behavior (no multi-assertion mega-tests)
expectations.evidence: TestTruncate_NoTruncationWhenWithinMaxLen has 2 assertions (lines 10-11) testing the same behavior with different inputs. TestTruncate_ZeroOrNegativeMaxLenReturnsEmptyString also has 2 assertions (lines 19-20).

expectations.text: Test names are descriptive sentences (e.g., TestTruncate_ReturnsOriginalWhenShorterThanLimit)
expectations.evidence: Names are descriptive: TestTruncate_NoTruncationWhenWithinMaxLen, TestWordWrap_EmptyInputReturnsEmptyString, etc.

expectations.text: Implementation uses minimal code to pass tests (no over-engineering)
expectations.evidence: Implementation has a correctness bug: Truncate returns s[:maxLen] + '...' (line 14), meaning Truncate('hello world', 5) returns 'hello...' (8 chars) — exceeds maxLen. The test expected this wrong behavior, suggesting tests didn't drive correct design.

expectations.text: Tests are run and confirmed failing before implementation is written
expectations.evidence: Transcript shows 'Running initial tests' (msg 9) and 'Running tests to catch undefined functions' (msg 11) before implementation.

expectations.text: All tests pass after implementation is complete
expectations.evidence: Transcript shows 'Confirming Go test success' (msg 16). Tests pass, though the Truncate test asserts incorrect behavior (result exceeds maxLen).

expectations.text: A regression test for the specific bug is written BEFORE any fix is applied
expectations.evidence: Agent confirmed red phase: test written first, go test failed before fix applied

expectations.text: The regression test name clearly describes the bug
expectations.evidence: TestParseDuration_FractionalHours — clearly describes the fractional hours bug

expectations.text: The regression test is run and confirmed to FAIL before the fix
expectations.evidence: Agent confirmed test failed on ParseDuration('1.5h') before fix

expectations.text: The fix uses strconv.ParseFloat instead of strconv.Atoi
expectations.evidence: duration.go:39 uses strconv.ParseFloat(numStr, 64)

expectations.text: The fix is minimal — only changes what's needed to handle fractional values
expectations.evidence: Only changed Atoi→ParseFloat and int→float64 multiplication. No other structural changes.

expectations.text: Existing behaviors are preserved (integer durations like '2h' still work)
expectations.evidence: Fix preserves integer behavior (ParseFloat handles integers). However no explicit regression test for integer durations.

expectations.text: Additional edge cases are tested (e.g., '0.5m', '2.25h', fractional seconds)
expectations.evidence: Only one test: TestParseDuration_FractionalHours testing '1.5h'. No tests for 0.5m, 2.25h, fractional seconds, or any other edge cases.

expectations.text: All tests pass after the fix
expectations.evidence: Agent confirmed go test ./... passes

expectations.text: Characterization tests for existing handler behavior are written first (valid request succeeds, invalid JSON returns 400)
expectations.evidence: No characterization tests. Agent jumped straight to validation tests. No test for existing 'invalid JSON returns 400' behavior or 'valid request creates user' as baseline before adding validation.

expectations.text: A test list or plan is created before writing validation tests
expectations.evidence: No test list or plan mentioned in agent output. Tests were written directly without a preceding plan.

expectations.text: Validation tests are written BEFORE validation code is added to the handler
expectations.evidence: Agent confirmed red phase: validation tests failed with 201 instead of 400 before handler changes were applied.

expectations.text: A fake or stub UserStore is created for testing (not a mock framework)
expectations.evidence: fakeUserStore struct at handler_test.go:15-18 with createFn callback pattern. No mock library imported.

expectations.text: Tests cover: invalid email format, empty name, name over 100 chars, password under 8 chars
expectations.evidence: TestHandleRegister_RejectsInvalidEmail, _RejectsEmptyName, _RejectsTooLongName, _RejectsShortPassword — all 4 validation rules covered.

expectations.text: Tests verify the HTTP status code and error message for each validation failure
expectations.evidence: Each test checks rec.Code == http.StatusBadRequest AND strings.Contains(rec.Body.String(), error message).

expectations.text: Valid requests still succeed after validation is added
expectations.evidence: TestHandleRegister_CreatesUserForValidInput checks StatusCreated, store.createCalled, and response body.

expectations.text: Each test tests one validation rule (not a single test checking all validations)
expectations.evidence: 5 separate test functions, each testing exactly one validation rule or the valid path.

expectations.text: Validation logic is clean — not deeply nested if/else chains
expectations.evidence: handler.go uses sequential if/return guards (lines 33-47). No nesting. Clean early-return pattern.

expectations.text: A test list or plan of test cases is created before any test code
expectations.evidence: strutil_test.go lines 9-16 contain a comment block listing all planned tests before any test code: '// Test list: // - returns original string when length is within maxLen // - truncates and adds ellipsis when input exceeds maxLen' etc.

expectations.text: Test file (strutil_test.go) is created before implementation file (strutil.go)
expectations.evidence: Transcript shows 'Creating initial failing tests' (msg 10) before 'Implementing minimal string utilities' (msg 15). Test file was written first.

expectations.text: Tests use Arrange-Act-Assert structure with clear sections
expectations.evidence: Each test has clear separation: variable assignment (arrange), function call on its own line (act), assert.Equal on its own line (assert). Example: TestTruncate_ReturnsOriginalWhenWithinMaxLen has result := Truncate (act) then assert.Equal (assert). No explicit AAA comments but structure is clear.

expectations.text: At least 5 distinct test cases for Truncate covering degenerate, happy, and edge cases
expectations.evidence: Only 3 Truncate tests: ReturnsOriginalWhenWithinMaxLen, TruncatesAndAddsEllipsis, HandlesVerySmallMaxLen. Missing: empty string, zero/negative maxLen, exact boundary (len == maxLen), string exactly 3 chars with maxLen 3.

expectations.text: At least 4 distinct test cases for WordWrap covering degenerate, happy, and edge cases
expectations.evidence: Only 3 WordWrap tests: WrapsAtWordBoundaries, ReturnsOriginalWhenNoWrapNeeded, HandlesLongWordOnOwnLine. Missing: empty string, single word input.

expectations.text: Each test function tests exactly one behavior (no multi-assertion mega-tests)
expectations.evidence: All 6 test functions test exactly one behavior with a single assertion each.

expectations.text: Test names are descriptive sentences (e.g., TestTruncate_ReturnsOriginalWhenShorterThanLimit)
expectations.evidence: All test names follow TestFunction_BehaviorDescription pattern: TestTruncate_ReturnsOriginalWhenWithinMaxLen, TestWordWrap_WrapsAtWordBoundaries, etc.

expectations.text: Implementation uses minimal code to pass tests (no over-engineering)
expectations.evidence: strutil.go is 46 lines, clean and minimal. Truncate handles edge cases (maxLen <= 0, maxLen <= 3) and the main case. WordWrap uses strings.Fields with simple line building. No unnecessary abstractions.

expectations.text: Tests are run and confirmed failing before implementation is written
expectations.evidence: Transcript shows 'Running failing tests to identify missing modules' (msg 11) and 'Verifying test failures on undefined functions' (msg 13) before 'Implementing minimal string utilities' (msg 15).

expectations.text: All tests pass after implementation is complete
expectations.evidence: Transcript shows 'Completing tests successfully' (msg 17). Implementation correctly handles all test cases including the tricky maxLen <= 3 edge case.

expectations.text: A regression test for the specific bug is written BEFORE any fix is applied
expectations.evidence: Agent confirmed RED phase: tests written first, strconv.Atoi parsing error confirmed before fix

expectations.text: The regression test name clearly describes the bug
expectations.evidence: Table-driven test with subtest 'fractional hours bug reproduction' — clearly describes the bug

expectations.text: The regression test is run and confirmed to FAIL before the fix
expectations.evidence: Agent confirmed: go test failed with 'strconv.Atoi: parsing 1.5: invalid syntax'

expectations.text: The fix uses strconv.ParseFloat instead of strconv.Atoi
expectations.evidence: duration.go:39 uses strconv.ParseFloat(numStr, 64)

expectations.text: The fix is minimal — only changes what's needed to handle fractional values
expectations.evidence: Only changed Atoi→ParseFloat and int→float64 multiplication. No other structural changes.

expectations.text: Existing behaviors are preserved (integer durations like '2h' still work)
expectations.evidence: Explicit 'integer regression' subtest: input '2h', want 2*time.Hour. Intentional regression guard.

expectations.text: Additional edge cases are tested (e.g., '0.5m', '2.25h', fractional seconds)
expectations.evidence: Table-driven test includes 4 cases: 1.5h (bug repro), 0.5m (fractional minutes), 2.25h (different fractional hours), 2h (integer regression).

expectations.text: All tests pass after the fix
expectations.evidence: Agent confirmed go test ./... passes

expectations.text: Characterization tests for existing handler behavior are written first (valid request succeeds, invalid JSON returns 400)
expectations.evidence: 3 characterization tests written and confirmed passing against unmodified handler: ValidRequest_Returns201WithUserJSON, InvalidJSON_Returns400, DatabaseError_Returns500. All 3 passed before any code changes.

expectations.text: A test list or plan is created before writing validation tests
expectations.evidence: Agent explicitly created test list: 'rejects invalid email format → 400', 'rejects empty name → 400', 'rejects whitespace-only name → 400', 'rejects name over 100 chars → 400', 'rejects password under 8 chars → 400', 'valid request still succeeds → 201'.

expectations.text: Validation tests are written BEFORE validation code is added to the handler
expectations.evidence: RED phase confirmed: all 6 validation tests failed with 'got 201' against unmodified handler. GREEN phase then added validateRegisterRequest() function.

expectations.text: A fake or stub UserStore is created for testing (not a mock framework)
expectations.evidence: stubUserStore struct at handler_test.go:16-18 with createUserFunc callback. No mock library imported.

expectations.text: Tests cover: invalid email format, empty name, name over 100 chars, password under 8 chars
expectations.evidence: All 4 rules covered plus bonus: InvalidEmail, EmptyName, WhitespaceOnlyName, NameOver100Chars, PasswordTooShort, PasswordExactly7Chars (boundary), PasswordExactly8Chars (boundary).

expectations.text: Tests verify the HTTP status code and error message for each validation failure
expectations.evidence: Tests verify HTTP status code (400) and that CreateUser was NOT called. However, error message text is NOT verified — assertValidationReject helper only checks rec.Code and createCalled, not response body content.

expectations.text: Valid requests still succeed after validation is added
expectations.evidence: Characterization test ValidRequest_Returns201WithUserJSON still passes. Additionally PasswordExactly8Chars_Returns201 verifies boundary valid case.

expectations.text: Each test tests one validation rule (not a single test checking all validations)
expectations.evidence: 7 separate test functions for validation, each testing exactly one scenario.

expectations.text: Validation logic is clean — not deeply nested if/else chains
expectations.evidence: Extracted validateRegisterRequest() function (handler.go:56-74) with sequential if/return guards. Clean separation of concerns.
